global_context.scene_description: A bold editorial cartoon-illustrated split-frame composition, single image with a clean vertical divider down the middle. Same homeowner character drawn in both frames — recognizable, cartoon proportions, expressive. Editorial cartoon illustration style with theatrical color blocking, NOT photorealistic, NOT flat infographic. LEFT FRAME (cool blue-grey background, anxious mood): the homeowner stands hunched over slightly, a comically tall WOBBLY STACK of dread-objects piled precariously on his head. The stack from bottom to top: a giant bold red invoice paper showing '$20,000', on top of that a calendar marked '2 WEEKS' with red X marks, on top of that a sleazy salesman cartoon caricature in a cheap suit holding a clipboard, on top of that a stack of bills/paperwork, on top of that a chunk of demolition debris, on top a tiny stress lightning bolt squiggle. The stack leans precariously, sweat drops fly off the homeowner's head, his eyes are bugged, eyebrows furrowed, hands raised to try to balance the stack. RIGHT FRAME (warm sunlit background, calm relieved mood): the SAME homeowner stands upright and relaxed, NOTHING on his head, holding ONE single clean ASH quote/document held up at chest level so the quote text is fully visible to the camera. The quote document clearly reads in large bold legible text: '$9,500' on one line, 'ONE DAY' on another line, with a small 'ASH COOLING & HEATING' header at the top. Eyebrows raised in pleasant surprise, small content smile, posture loose, weight evenly distributed. Above the entire image, a clean banner-style headline reads in bold uppercase: 'REPLACING YOUR AC ISN'T WHAT YOU THINK.' At the bottom, a small italic caption: 'Cheaper. Faster. Easier than your neighbor's horror story.' A bold black vertical line separates the two halves. Mood: the visible relief of dropping a stack of imagined fears that turn out to be wrong by 2x or more. Editorial cartoon illustration, theatrical color blocking, expressive character work.
